summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a regular session addressing old and new business. Old business included rescinding a prior resolution prepared in error, granting continuances for Jeffrey Kelly cases, and approving resolutions for Don Cornwell's patio and deck variances contingent on lot consolidation. New business included approving a setback variance for an unpermitted pavilion at 6334 Liberty Lane, a use variance for temporary RV living at 2439 Woodville Pike during home construction, and a lot coverage variance for a shed at 6005 Mark Circle.
decisions: Approved motion to rescind Resolution R-032-22 (denial of PUD variance prepared in error)
Approved continuance of BZA 2022-011 to October 17, 2022
Approved continuance of BZA 2022-012 to October 17, 2022
Approved Resolution R-045-2022 for Don Cornwell variance (patio) at 6607 State Route 48 with lot consolidation condition
Approved Resolution for Don Cornwell variance (deck) at 6607 State Route 48 with lot consolidation condition
Approved BZA 2022-014 area variance for accessory structure at 6334 Liberty Lane
Approved BZA 2022-016 use variance for temporary RV living at 2439 Woodville Pike until final building permit issued
Approved BZA 2022-018 lot coverage variance for shed at 6005 Mark Circle
Motion to close the meeting approved
action_items: Harry to prepare resolutions for BZA 2022-014, BZA 2022-016, and BZA 2022-018 for the next meeting
Harry's office to continue revised policy of not posting checks or receipts with financial information on the website
Harry working with Board of Trustees on text amendment to allow RV temporary use on vacant lots with building permits
Scott Humphreys/Amy Campbell to remove RVs from 2439 Woodville Pike upon receiving final building permit
key_amounts: 10 foot minimum side yard setback requirement per Article 5, Table 5-2
4 foot 8 inch variance requested for pavilion setback at 6334 Liberty Lane
14 by 24 foot accessory structure permitted at 6334 Liberty Lane
Two 350 square foot RVs proposed at 2439 Woodville Pike
0.137 acre lot size at 6005 Mark Circle
0.42 ISR calculated vs 0.30 allowed for lot coverage at 6005 Mark Circle
30% lot coverage allowed for lots half acre and below
40% lot coverage for lots half acre to one acre
50% lot coverage for lots over one acre
$280,000 to $500,000 price range for new subdivision homes
12 homes sold in new Ryan Homes subdivision off Route 48
notable_topics: Continuances for Jeffrey Kelly cases to October 17
Don Cornwell lot consolidation and variance approvals for patio and deck at 6607 State Route 48
Variance for unpermitted pavilion at 6334 Liberty Lane (8-inch setback deficiency)
Privacy breach - applicant's bank account information accidentally posted on township website
Use variance for temporary RV living during home construction at 2439 Woodville Pike
Lot coverage variance for shed at 6005 Mark Circle
Text amendment in progress to allow temporary RV use with building permits
New 310-home subdivision development discussion with connectivity to schools and parks
Alternate board member vacancy still open
notes: A significant privacy breach occurred when the township accidentally posted an applicant's bank routing and account information from a scanned check on the public website. The affected residents (Ruseys) had to open new bank accounts and redirect all automatic deposits and debits. The township implemented a new policy to no longer post checks or receipts. The September BZA meeting was skipped due to staff vacation, with next meeting scheduled for October 17, 2022. Discussion at end of meeting included details about a major 310-home subdivision development near the school with plans for connectivity, sidewalks, and park access.
